name: Grandma's Oatmeal Cookies
recipeInstructions:
1. Combine 1 cup boiling water with raisins and 1 teaspoon baking soda in a bowl. Stir well and set aside.
2. Bring shortening to room temperature, then cream well. Add sugar one cup at a time, creaming after each addition.
3. Add eggs in the same manner as sugar.
4. Sift together cinnamon, cloves, allspice, flour, and baking powder into a separate bowl.
5. Stir vanilla into the creamed mixture.
6. Drain raisins and sprinkle one tablespoon of flour over them; shake to coat.
7. Alternately add the sifted ingredients, buttermilk, and oats, mixing well after each addition.
8. Mix in walnuts and raisins with a heavy wooden spoon until thoroughly incorporated.
9. Grease a cookie sheet and drop teaspoonfuls of dough onto it (about 20 per sheet).
10. Bake at 350°F for 10-12 minutes; cookies should be golden on the bottom and dry on top.
11. Remove from oven and place on wax paper to cool.
12. Store in an airtight container, such as Tupperware.
Makes about 10 dozen. Variation: Use the water saved from soaking raisins and reduce milk accordingly for enhanced flavor.
